item.title: Introduction to Topological Data Analysis
item.description:
Module 1: structures and invariants

Homework 1

The credit from the lecture part will be given on the basis of a project which you are supposed to select till the end April. The presentation of your projects will be held on during classes on a fixed day. You can get maximal 100 points for the project. In order to get the maximal number of points you are required to:

  • Present your project (up to 100 points.)
  • Pass the Exercises/LAB sessions with a positive mark (details given below).

During the exercises and LAB sessions to Introduction to Topological Data Analysis you can obtain 110 points (110%). In order to get the maximal number of points, you are required to:

  • Present the mini project (up to 50 points)
  • Solve homeworks (up to 50 points): you will be given five sets of exercises. In order to get the perfect score on that part, approximately three sets are required to be solved.
  • Active participation in the classes (up to 10 points, maximal one point per a 90 min meeting). The participation in exercises/LABs is compulsory and it is allowed to have three justified absences.

Grade scale: 50% dst (3), 60% dst+ (3.5), 70% db (4), 80% db+ (4.5), 90% bdb (5)

item.title: Wstęp do algebry i teorii liczb
item.description:
Sylabus

Zasady zaliczenia

Literatura:

(1) A. Iwaszkiewicz-Rudoszańska, Wstęp do algebry i teorii liczb, Wydawnictwo Naukowe UAM, Poznań 2009.

(2) J. Rutkowski, Teoria liczb w zadaniach, Wydawnictwo Naukowe PWN, Warszawa 2018.

(3) J. Rutkowski, Algebra abstrakcyjna w zadaniach, Wydawnictwo Naukowe PWN, Warszawa 2006.

item.title: Modern methods of data analysis
item.description: A course conducted for PhD students of International Environmental Doctoral School.

item.title: Modern methods of data analysis
item.description: A course conducted for PhD students of International Environmental Doctoral School.

item.title: Basics of mathematics
item.description: A course conducted for Cumputer Science undegraduate students of Adam Mickiewicz University in Poznań. The course included basics of logic, set theory, calculus and probability theory.

item.title: Introduction to mathematics
item.description: A course conducted for Mathematics undegraduate students of Adam Mickiewicz University in Poznań. The course included basics of logic, set theory, calculus and probability theory.

item.title: Review of elementary mathematics
item.description: A course conducted for the first year Mathematics undegraduate students of Adam Mickiewicz University in Poznań. The course aimed at the repetition of the topics tought in high schools.
